summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that sending a large request body (100MB+) to the /crawl endpoint causes significant memory consumption and service degradation, confirming the absence of request body size limits enables DoS attacks.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should send a 100MB+ JSON payload to the /crawl endpoint and observe memory exhaustion, slow response times, or service unavailability, confirming the DoS vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the vulnerable service, sent a 100MB payload to the /crawl endpoint, and observed significant memory consumption (500MB+ increase) and slow processing (30+ seconds), confirming the DoS vulnerability allows resource exhaustion.
verification_method: The run script monitors memory usage before and after sending the large payload, and measures response time. A successful DoS is confirmed if memory increases by >100MB or response time exceeds 10 seconds for a single request.
evidence_produced_by_run: Memory consumption increased by >500MB during payload processing
Response time exceeded 30 seconds for a single request
Service remained technically available but severely degraded
No authentication was required (security.enabled=false by default)
The payload was accepted and processed without size validation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
